sutta: once when the exalted 1 was dwelling near savathi at jaita grove in anata pindika spa a mother and son were both spending the rainy season in savathi as monk and none they longed to see 1 another often the mother often wished for her son and the son his mother and from seeing each other often companionship arose from companionship intimacy from intimacy amorousness and without giving up the training and making their weakness manifest with their hearts inflamed they gave themselves over to incestuous intercourse and a company of monks went to the exalted 1 saluted him and sat down at 1 side and so seated they told the exalted 1 all that had occurred in the buddha sid what monks knows not this foolish man that a mother shall not lust after her son nor son verily after his mother monks i see no other single form so enticing so desirable so intoxicating so binding so distracting such a hindrance to winning the unsurpassed peace from effort that is to say amongst as a woman's form monks whosoever clings to a woman's form infatuated greedy fettered enslaved enthralled for many a long day shall grieve snared by the charms of a woman's form monks i know no other single sound no other single smell tastes touch so enticing so desirable so intoxicating so binding so distracting such a hindrance to winning the unsurpassed peace from effort that is to say monks as the sound smell taste and touch of a woman monks whosoever clings to the sound smell taste and touch of a woman infatuated greedy fettered enslaved enthralled for many a long day shall grieve snared by a woman's charms monks a woman even when going along will stop to ensnare the heart of a man whether standing sitting or lying down laughing talking or singing weeping stricken or dying a woman will stop to ensnare the heart of a man monks if ever 1 would rightly say it is wholly a snare of mara verily speaking rightly 1 may say a womanhood it is wholly a snare of mara there's a trap of mara that's the end of the suta
commentary: this suta what the buddha has just said is equally applicable to uh man also and the conversa because in this instance the buddha was addressing the monks so he was saying that nothing um can entice a monk or a man nothing is so intoxicating as a woman and the converse is also true to a woman nothing is so desirable so intoxicating so exciting as a man and so just as a woman is a trap for a man so a man is also a trap for a woman that is why 1 of the greatest obstacles to the holy life the spiritual path is the attraction of the opposite sex it is nature's way to procreate the species so we have to be very careful when we want to walk the spiritual path that is why 1 of the requisites of the holy life for renuncians is that they become celibate they have to take vows or the precept of chastity this is uh 1 of the most difficult uh obstacles to overcome in the holy life this is why i've mentioned before a lot of monks this role because of a woman and i suppose the converse is also true now the next suta
